name: backup_restore
description: A backup/restore lifecycle world with a Recovery Point Objective: the clock advances each tick, widening the data-loss window until a verified recovery point is re-established. Actions: backup, verify, restore.

card.description: A backup/restore lifecycle world with a Recovery Point Objective: the clock advances each tick, widening the data-loss window until a verified recovery point is re-established. Actions: backup, verify, restore.

rules: Every action advances the clock by one tick; ongoing writes widen the data-loss window since the last trusted recovery point.
'backup' captures the live data at the current time as a new, UNVERIFIED restore point.
'verify' validates the latest backup; only a verified backup becomes the trusted recovery point (last_verified_time).
'restore' is only possible from a trusted recovery point; it recovers the system to that point and collapses exposure to zero for that tick.
exposure = time - last_verified_time (or time if nothing is verified); an RPO violation occurs whenever exposure exceeds rpo_target.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action["name"] if isinstance(action, dict) else action

    # Time always advances: ongoing writes accumulate recovery-point exposure.
    s["time"] = s["time"] + 1

    if name == "backup":
        # Capture the live data now as a new, not-yet-trusted restore point.
        s["last_backup_time"] = s["time"]
        s["backup_verified"] = False
        s["backups_taken"] = s["backups_taken"] + 1
        s["status"] = "operational"
    elif name == "verify":
        # Validate the latest backup; only an unverified, existing backup can be
        # verified, and verification promotes it to the trusted recovery point.
        if s["last_backup_time"] >= 0 and not s["backup_verified"]:
            s["backup_verified"] = True
            s["backups_verified"] = s["backups_verified"] + 1
            s["last_verified_time"] = s["last_backup_time"]
    elif name == "restore":
        # Recover only from a trusted (verified) recovery point. After recovery
        # the live data matches that point, so exposure collapses for this tick.
        if s["last_verified_time"] >= 0:
            s["restores"] = s["restores"] + 1
            s["status"] = "restored"
            s["last_verified_time"] = s["time"]

    # Recompute the data-loss window and RPO compliance.
    if s["last_verified_time"] >= 0:
        s["exposure"] = s["time"] - s["last_verified_time"]
    else:
        s["exposure"] = s["time"]
    s["rpo_violation"] = s["exposure"] > s["rpo_target"]
    return s

emit.report: t={time} status={status} | last backup @ {last_backup_time} (verified={backup_verified}) | trusted recovery point @ {last_verified_time} | exposure={exposure}/{rpo_target} rpo_violation={rpo_violation} | backups={backups_taken} verified={backups_verified} restores={restores}
